_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Support target list management (i.e. Restricted Target List (RTL), Joint Target List (JTL), Candidate Target List (CTL), etc.).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of dbt (data build tool) or equivalent transformation framework patterns, including model layering (staging, intermediate, mart), testing, documentation, and lineage generation.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-T-008 (TF, cosine: 0.363), TF-T-001 (TF, cosine: 0.356).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 1.6/10 (raw 0.159). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [candidate, ctl, etc, i, joint, jtl] vs WIDAI-only terms [build, data, dbt, documentation, equivalent, framework] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.826 (P=0.816, R=0.837). R exceeds P by 0.022 — the WIDAI KSA's 22 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 18 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:3.3%, N:56.6%, C:40.1%). No dominant classification for "Support target list management (i.e. Restricted Target List (RTL), Joint Target" vs "Knowledge of dbt (data build tool) or equivalent transformation framework patter" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.364 > STS 0.159 (gap +0.205). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [candidate, ctl, etc, i, joint, jtl]. WIDAI terms: [build, data, dbt, documentation, equivalent, framework].
